origin.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
origin.key_document: Acte de deces (death certificate in French, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Democratic Republic of the Congo travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Kinshasa guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation is not widely available in the DRC. Full body repatriation is the standard approach.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Acte de deces (certified English translation required)
special_rules: The FCDO advises against all travel to many parts of the DRC. Contact the FCDO emergency line immediately: +44 (0)20 7008 5000.
All French-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
Limited infrastructure and bureaucratic processes extend timelines significantly.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Kinshasa can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: DR Congo to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Repatriation takes 3-6 weeks. French translation, police clearance, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
